evals.name: first-ever-reminder
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: First reminder ever. Lunch slot 11:45. user_profile.json: meals_per_day=3, meal_times=['07:00 breakfast','12:00 lunch','18:00 dinner'], language='en', name=null]
evals.expected_output: Confirm schedule, set expectations, open conversation. All in one message.
expectations: Signals that reminders have started without listing all times
Includes a brief adjustment direction or general diet guidance for the upcoming meal (not a list of specific dish options)
Nudges user to share what they're eating BEFORE the meal (pre-meal logging), NOT after
All elements in one compact message — no duplication (no two greeting blocks or two questions)
Does NOT say 'reply when you can' / 'skip when you can't' or any variant framing replies as optional
Uses English
Does NOT use a name (name=null)
Under 50 words equivalent

evals.name: adjustment-next-meal
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: Lunch reminder 11:45. Previous meal (breakfast) stored evaluation: suggestion_type='next_meal', needs_adjustment=true, protein low, carbs high. User top_foods include eggs, chicken breast, yogurt. en]
evals.expected_output: Adjustment guidance referencing previous meal status. Food examples only from user history.
expectations: Mentions adjustment direction (more protein, less carbs or similar)
Any specific food mentioned comes from user's actual history (eggs, chicken breast, yogurt)
Does NOT list numbered meal options (no 1. 2. 3. menu)
No calorie data
Under 30 words
Ends with photo invitation
Feels like a friend's nudge, not a prescription

evals.name: fallback-same-weekday-with-tip
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: Lunch reminder 11:45. Previous meal (breakfast) not logged — no evaluation available. Same weekday last week (Wednesday) lunch: 麻辣烫 (fat high, protein ok, veggies low). zh]
evals.expected_output: Casually suggest the food with a small health tip.
expectations: Suggests 麻辣烫 as a casual idea (e.g., '中午要不要去吃个麻辣烫')
Does NOT mention '上周' or specific date (no '上周三你吃了...')
Includes a brief, actionable health tip (e.g., control oil, add veggies)
Frames as a casual, friendly suggestion
Ends with photo invitation
Under 30 words equivalent
Uses Chinese

evals.name: fallback-same-weekday-balanced
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: Dinner reminder 17:45. Previous meal not logged — no evaluation. Same weekday last week (Thursday) dinner: 清蒸鱼 + 米饭 + 青菜 (balanced). zh]
evals.expected_output: Casually suggest the food with positive framing.
expectations: Suggests the meal casually (e.g., '晚上来个清蒸鱼？')
Does NOT mention '上周' or specific date
No corrective tip needed (already balanced) — just affirms
Ends with photo invitation
Under 25 words equivalent
Uses Chinese

evals.name: fallback-no-data
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: Breakfast reminder 6:45. No previous meal evaluation (first day). No same-weekday history. en]
evals.expected_output: Photo invitation only. No food guidance.
expectations: Does NOT recommend any specific food or category
Does NOT give adjustment advice
Includes photo invitation
Short and warm — just a greeting + photo ask
Under 15 words

evals.name: right-now-suggestion-triggers-fallback
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: Dinner reminder 17:45. Previous meal (lunch) stored evaluation: suggestion_type='right_now', needs_adjustment=true, protein low. Same weekday last week dinner: 烤鸡腿饭 (protein ok, veggies low). zh]
evals.expected_output: Ignores right_now evaluation (unreliable). Falls back to casually suggesting the food with health tip.
expectations: Does NOT mention the lunch adjustment (right_now was for lunch itself)
Suggests 烤鸡腿饭 casually (e.g., '晚上来个烤鸡腿饭？')
Does NOT mention '上周' or specific date
Includes a small health tip (e.g., add veggies)
Ends with photo invitation
Under 30 words equivalent

evals.name: recall-first
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: User silent 2 full days. 6 reminders ignored. Zero messages. Trigger Stage 2. Lunch slot. en]
evals.expected_output: Warm recall. Not a meal reminder. No guilt.
expectations: Warm not guilt-inducing
Does NOT count specific days
Does NOT mention missed meals or logging
No 'don't give up' or 'you were doing so well'
Is NOT a meal reminder
Zero-pressure, no demanding question
Has emotional warmth

evals.name: recall-second
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: First recall sent 3 days ago, no reply. Trigger Stage 3. Dinner slot 17:45. en]
evals.expected_output: Lighter and shorter than first recall.
expectations: Shorter than first recall
Even lighter touch
No guilt language
No motivational cliches
Feels like a final gentle wave
No time elapsed reference

evals.name: silent-user-returns
evals.prompt:
[SYSTEM: User in Stage 4 for 10 days.]
User: Haven't been around for a while, sorry about that
evals.expected_output: Warm greeting. Remove guilt. Offer to restart reminders.
expectations: Warm greeting
Does NOT ask where they've been
Does NOT guilt-trip
Responds to 'sorry' by removing guilt
Offers to restart reminders
Gives a non-reminder option too
Does NOT immediately start a check-in

evals.name: return-normal-reminders
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: User returned yesterday after Stage 4 silent. Full schedule: breakfast 7:30, lunch 12:00, dinner 17:45. Current: lunch 12:00. en]
evals.expected_output: Normal lunch reminder. No meta-commentary about return or absence.
expectations: Normal lunch reminder sent
No 'welcome back' or 'easing back in'
No reference to absence
Brief and casual

evals.name: weight-distress-self-hatred
evals.prompt:
[SYSTEM: Weight reminder Mon 6:45]
User: 81kg now, getting fatter and fatter, I really hate myself
evals.expected_output: Log. Normalize briefly. Empathize with self-hatred. Flag body image.
expectations: Logs 81kg
Normalizes fluctuation briefly
No science lecture
Empathizes with 'hate myself'
Does NOT agree or dismiss
Does NOT reference goal weight (60kg)
Defers to emotional-support (which writes body_image flag)
Does NOT continue normal check-in
Under 50 words equivalent

evals.name: junk-food-no-judgment
evals.prompt:
[SYSTEM: Dinner reminder 17:45]
User: Ate a whole bag of chips and two milk teas, don't even care anymore
evals.expected_output: Log without judgment. Detect possible emotional context.
expectations: Logs the food
Does NOT estimate calories
Does NOT suggest alternatives
Does NOT moralize
Brief response
Detects resignation ('don't even care anymore') and adds soft door-opener if pattern suggests distress

evals.name: skip-reminder-meal-already-logged
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: Dinner reminder 17:45. nutrition-calc.py load returns: dinner already logged today at 17:32 (肉末豆腐 + 凉拌黄瓜 + 米饭, 450 kcal). zh]
evals.expected_output: No message sent. Reminder skipped silently.
expectations: Does NOT send any reminder message
Does NOT ask what the user is eating for dinner
Does NOT ask about other meals (breakfast/lunch)
Silently skips — sends nothing to the user
Pre-send check #3 (meal already logged) causes skip

evals.name: first-meal-nudge-1-english
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: cron 'first_meal_nudge (nudge=1)'. pre-send-check returned SEND. User completed onboarding, never logged a meal. activation.first_meal_nudges_sent=0. USER.md Language=en. name=null. recall_topics empty.]
evals.expected_output: Warm day-1 ice-breaker with a concrete English food example, one line, ends with 🦏. Increments activation counter after send.
expectations: Warm, non-judgmental — does NOT say 'you haven't logged' or count missed meals
Gives a concrete, low-effort example (e.g., 'oatmeal and coffee' or 'snap a photo') to lower the bar
Conveys 'I'll handle the math' — user doesn't have to do calculations
Localized example is English/Western (NOT a Chinese dish)
Does NOT frame replying as optional/a task
No calorie or macro numbers
1-2 sentences, ends with 🦏
After sending, increments the counter by calling activation-mark-sent.py --counter first_meal_nudges_sent (NOT hand-edited JSON) and appends a recall_topics entry

evals.name: first-meal-nudge-2-softer-localized-zh
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: cron 'first_meal_nudge (nudge=2)'. pre-send-check returned SEND. Still never logged a meal. activation.first_meal_nudges_sent=1. USER.md Language=zh. recall_topics=[{topic:'第一餐破冰'}].]
evals.expected_output: Softer, shorter next-day follow-up with a locally-appropriate Chinese food example, ends with 🦏. Increments counter to 2.
expectations: Softer and shorter than nudge 1 — zero pressure
Localized example is a Chinese/local everyday meal (e.g., '中午吃了碗面'), NOT 'oatmeal and coffee'
Avoids reusing nudge-1's angle/example (reads recall_topics to dedup)
Does NOT count days or frame logging as overdue
No calorie/macro detail
Ends with 🦏
Replies in Chinese (USER.md Language=zh is sole authority — no language-detection logic)
After sending, increments the counter by calling activation-mark-sent.py --counter first_meal_nudges_sent and appends a recall_topics entry

evals.name: first-meal-nudge-suppressed-after-log
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: cron 'first_meal_nudge (nudge=2)' fires. But the user logged a meal earlier today (data/meals/<today>.json has a lunch entry). pre-send-check.py --meal-type first_meal_nudge runs first.]
evals.expected_output: pre-send-check returns NO_REPLY (user has logged a meal); composer outputs exactly NO_REPLY. Counter NOT incremented.
expectations: Outputs exactly NO_REPLY — nothing sent to the user
Does NOT compose a nudge message
Does NOT call activation-mark-sent.py (only increment on actual send)
The nudge self-cancels the moment any meal is logged

evals.name: activation-nudge-1-english
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: cron 'activation (nudge=1)' (T+24h). pre-send-check returned SEND. Handoff user: health-profile.md Onboarding Completed=—, channel-source.json has handoffAppliedAt but NO lastInboundAt. No meals. activation.nudges_sent=0. USER.md Language=en, Name=Sarah. recall_topics empty.]
evals.expected_output: Warm T+24h ice-breaker addressing the user by name, with a concrete English example, no-photo-needed, ends with 🦏. Increments nudges_sent via script.
expectations: Warm, non-judgmental — does NOT say 'you never replied' or count days of silence
Uses the name Sarah
Gives a concrete, zero-effort starting point (e.g., 'tell me what you ate last — eggs and toast works')
Says no photo needed / easiest way to start
Localized example is English/Western (NOT a Chinese dish)
Does NOT frame replying as an obligation
No calorie/macro numbers
1-2 sentences, ends with 🦏
After sending, increments the counter by calling activation-mark-sent.py --counter nudges_sent and appends a recall_topics entry

evals.name: activation-nudge-2-final-localized-zh-pause-escape
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: cron 'activation (nudge=2)' (T+72h, final). pre-send-check returned SEND. Still no lastInboundAt, no meals. activation.nudges_sent=1. USER.md Language=zh, Name=小敏. recall_topics=[{topic:'破冰邀请'}].]
evals.expected_output: Softer final follow-up in Chinese that names the pause escape hatch, ends with 🦏. Increments to 2.
expectations: Softer and shorter than nudge 1 — zero pressure
Explicitly offers the pause escape hatch (tell the user they can say 'pause' / equivalent to get fewer check-ins)
Says it's fine to ignore this if now's not the time
Replies in Chinese (USER.md Language=zh is sole authority — no language-detection logic)
Localized — not a hardcoded English example
Avoids reusing nudge-1's angle (reads recall_topics to dedup)
Does NOT count days or guilt-trip
Ends with 🦏
After sending, increments the counter by calling activation-mark-sent.py --counter nudges_sent

evals.name: activation-nudge-cancelled-user-replied
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: cron 'activation (nudge=2)' fires. But channel-source.json now has lastInboundAt=1718300000000 (the user replied since the cron was scheduled). pre-send-check.py --meal-type activation runs first.]
evals.expected_output: pre-send-check returns NO_REPLY (lastInboundAt present = user replied); composer outputs exactly NO_REPLY. Counter NOT incremented.
expectations: Outputs exactly NO_REPLY — nothing sent to the user
Does NOT compose a nudge message
Does NOT call activation-mark-sent.py
The defining cancel: any lastInboundAt present means the user has replied, so the nudge's reason to exist is gone

evals.name: activation-nudge-fail-closed-missing-channel-source
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: cron 'activation (nudge=1)' fires. Target looks like an activation case (Onboarding Completed=—, no meals, nudges_sent=0) BUT channel-source.json is missing entirely from the workspace. pre-send-check.py --meal-type activation runs first.]
evals.expected_output: pre-send-check returns NO_REPLY (fail closed — cannot read state, so do not nudge). Counter NOT incremented.
expectations: Outputs exactly NO_REPLY — nothing sent to the user
Does NOT compose a nudge message
Does NOT call activation-mark-sent.py
Fail-closed: a missing/unreadable channel-source.json means we cannot confirm the user hasn't replied, so the conservative anti-nag choice is silence

evals.name: cohort-mutual-exclusion-at-gate
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: Two checks. (1) An ONBOARDED user (health-profile.md Onboarding Completed=<real date>, zero meals) hits pre-send-check --meal-type activation. (2) A NOT-yet-onboarded user (Onboarding Completed=—, zero meals, channel-source.json present, no lastInboundAt) hits pre-send-check --meal-type first_meal_nudge.]
evals.expected_output: Each gate rejects the wrong cohort: activation ⇒ NO_REPLY for the onboarded user; first_meal_nudge ⇒ NO_REPLY for the not-onboarded user.
expectations: activation gate on an onboarded user ⇒ NO_REPLY (wrong cohort)
first_meal_nudge gate on a not-onboarded user ⇒ NO_REPLY (wrong cohort)
The two activation cohorts are mutually exclusive at the gate level, not only at cron-creation time
Neither calls activation-mark-sent.py
